symptom: MDP templates with duplicate exact keys, underscore/hyphen alias collisions, malformed text lines, or malformed .ndx group-body tokens could be silently reinterpreted before patching and could drive unsafe auto tc-grps decisions.
root_cause: The read path used implicit text decoding and plain dict overwrites, so input validation happened later than write-side cleanup, while .ndx group-size parsing silently ignored non-integer tokens and auto-mode threshold sanitization was duplicated and incomplete for invalid or non-finite values.
trigger: An MDP template contains duplicate exact keys or semantic alias variants, a malformed non-comment line, or an index group body contains non-integer junk while tc-grps auto mode depends on parsed Polymer/NonPolymer sizes.
fix: Use one explicit UTF-8 MDP read path, normalize duplicate exact and semantic keys during parse with strict/non-strict diagnostics, preserve malformed-line strict failures, reject malformed .ndx group-body tokens, and centralize tc-grps threshold sanitization before auto/split decisions.
prevention: Use one parser/decoder path for both template reads and post-write validation.
Detect duplicate exact keys and semantic alias collisions before patching, not only during final write cleanup.
Treat malformed .ndx group-body tokens as unsafe whenever thermostat decisions depend on parsed group sizes.
Sanitize invalid or non-finite safety thresholds in one helper before comparing group sizes/fractions.
notes: DiagnosticSink was left unchanged because the current code did not expose a concrete same-instance cross-phase contamination bug.

symptom: Pressure-coupling validation lost missing or unknown pcoupltype diagnostics whenever pcoupl was enabled but ref_p was absent, so incomplete NPT MDPs could fail to explain why pressure-coupling state was unsafe or ambiguous.
root_cause: _validate_ref_p_dimensionality stored pcoupltype diagnostics in a local DiagnosticSink but returned early on missing ref_p before merging that sink into the function-level warnings/errors.
trigger: An MDP enables pcoupl, omits ref_p, and also omits pcoupltype or uses an unknown pcoupltype value.
fix: Flush the local DiagnosticSink before the missing-ref_p early return so pcoupltype diagnostics survive even when ref_p dimensionality checks are skipped.
prevention: When validation helpers accumulate diagnostics in a local sink, merge them before every early return.
Add regression tests for incomplete-input paths, not only the fully populated validation path.
Keep strict and non-strict diagnostic plumbing aligned even when a downstream value is missing.
notes: This pass intentionally left three newly added guardrails as strict xfails for later one-bug-at-a-time repairs: temperature/gen_temp ordering, non-strict split without tau_t, and single-token comm-grps ndx validation.

symptom: A successful `gmx_em` run could leave no `done.ok`, so direct stage execution and later resume/skip checks could treat a completed EM stage as incomplete.
root_cause: The EM stage relied on the outer stage wrapper to emit the success sentinel, but the stage implementation itself owned the artifact/state persistence ordering used by direct stage invocations and regression tests.
trigger: `GmxEmStage.run()` completes successfully and writes `stage_state.json`/`repro.json`, but the caller exercises `run()` directly instead of going through the outer dispatcher wrapper.
fix: Write `done.ok` inside `GmxEmStage.run()` after `stage_state.json`, `repro.json`, and `run_report.json` are persisted.
prevention: If a stage runner can be invoked directly or owns skip logic, emit its final sentinel inside the runner after durable metadata writes.
Keep regression tests for both successful completion and sentinel-gated skip behavior.
Do not mark a GROMACS stage complete before `stage_state.json` and `repro.json` exist on disk.

symptom: A successful `gmx_prod` run could finish without `done.ok` in both fresh-start and same-stage resume success paths, breaking deterministic completion and skip semantics.
root_cause: The production stage persisted state and audit metadata but never emitted its own final sentinel in the stage-owned success branches.
trigger: `GmxProdStage.run()` completes successfully from either a fresh NPT checkpoint handoff or a same-stage `md.cpt` resume path.
fix: Write `done.ok` inside both successful `GmxProdStage.run()` completion branches after state, repro, and run-report artifacts are durable.
prevention: Keep separate regression coverage for fresh-complete and resume-complete production success paths.
Write stage completion sentinels only after the last required metadata file is persisted.
Do not assume an outer wrapper is the only code path that will drive stage completion.

symptom: The first normal same-stage production resume could be denied by a false fingerprint mismatch even when persisted stage inputs and commands still matched.
root_cause: The resume path rebuilt `stage_state.json` without carrying forward the recorded `velocity_mode`, so the recomputed compatibility payload dropped a semantic field present in the persisted payload.
trigger: A production stage is resumed from existing `md.cpt`/`md.tpr`/`stage_state.json`, and the recorded payload contains `velocity_mode` from the original run.
fix: Reuse the recorded `velocity_mode` during resume compatibility recomputation and when refreshing `stage_state.json` after successful continuation.
prevention: Resume fingerprint recomputation must preserve recorded semantic mode fields, not only file hashes and commands.
Add same-stage resume regressions that compare persisted and recomputed payloads before `mdrun` is launched.
Treat `stage_state.json` as the source of truth for prior restart semantics during resume validation.

symptom: Mutually exclusive restart flags could raise an uncaught `ValueError` from `decide_prev_state_policy()` and bypass normal stage-failure semantics.
root_cause: The NPT and production stage callers delegated restart-policy selection to `decide_prev_state_policy()` but did not convert its configuration `ValueError` into a controlled stage-level failure.
trigger: Programmatic or test-level stage execution reaches NPT or production with both `--allow-velocity-reset` and `--allow-gro-velocity-restart` enabled while the prior checkpoint is missing.
fix: Catch `ValueError` from `decide_prev_state_policy()` in the NPT and production stage callers, print the restart-policy guidance, and return `False` like other stage failures.
prevention: Stage wrappers around restart-policy helpers must convert configuration exceptions into normal stage failures.
Keep direct stage-call regressions for invalid recovery-flag combinations even if CLI parsing already rejects them.
Do not let helper exceptions bypass crash-context and pipeline failure semantics.

symptom: A `.gro` file with syntactically present but numerically all-zero velocity columns could be accepted as a valid restart source, allowing near-0 K restarts to masquerade as velocity-preserving resumes.
root_cause: The GRO velocity classifier only checked that sampled velocity columns parsed as floats; it never required any sampled velocity component to be materially non-zero.
trigger: Restart policy validates a `.gro` file whose sampled atom lines contain `0.0000 0.0000 0.0000` velocity triplets (or values within numeric tolerance of zero).
fix: Require parsed sampled velocity components to exceed a numeric non-zero tolerance before a GRO is considered a reusable velocity restart source.
prevention: Velocity validation must check physical content, not only parseability.
Treat syntactically present all-zero restart velocities as missing restart state.
Keep helper-level and policy-level regressions for zero-velocity and near-zero-velocity GRO inputs.

symptom: Wrapper launches such as `mpirun ... gmx_mpi` could record launcher paths in the main GROMACS executable provenance fields, making audit output misleading.
root_cause: The provenance helper treated the first command token as both launcher and `gmx_cmd_used`, so wrapper binaries and actual GROMACS binaries were conflated.
trigger: A stage command is launched through a wrapper such as `mpirun`, `srun`, or similar before the actual `gmx`/`gmx_mpi` token.
fix: Resolve `gmx_cmd_used` and `gmx_bin_resolved` from the actual GROMACS token while keeping separate launcher token/path fields for the wrapper.
prevention: When wrapped launchers are allowed, record launcher and actual binary provenance in distinct fields.
Prefer the extracted `gmx`/`gmx_mpi` token for version probes and executable hashes.
Add regression tests for wrapped command strings, not only plain `gmx` invocations.

symptom: The NPT equilibrium metrics output exposed only `last_window_ps`, so operators could mistake the 20 ps tail-average as a robust equilibration verdict instead of a short diagnostic preview.
root_cause: The metrics writer preserved the configurable/actual window calculation but did not emit explicit metadata describing the requested window or the diagnostic-only meaning of the tail-window summary.
trigger: `gmx_eq` writes `stage_metrics.json` after NPT and downstream QC or operators inspect the file without any explicit window-semantics metadata.
fix: Keep the existing default/configurable window behavior, but add `window_ps_configured` and `window_semantics=diagnostic_tail_window` metadata and note the diagnostic tail-window role in the written metrics.
prevention: When short-window metrics are used for QC previews, record both the configured window and the semantic role in machine-readable output.
Prefer explicit metadata over silent changes to scientific meaning.
Add regressions for both default-window fallback and custom-window requests.

symptom: Same-stage production resume could succeed after the newly computed compatibility payload silently dropped a recorded fingerprint field, because schema drift only emitted a warning and the gate compared shared keys only.
root_cause: The MD resume gate intersected recorded/current payload keys on schema mismatch instead of failing closed, so a missing semantic field such as `velocity_mode` could be excluded from comparison and the stage would continue.
trigger: `stage_state.json` contains a recorded MD resume-gate field, but the newly computed `resume_gate_payload` omits that field during a same-stage production resume.
fix: Deny production resume on unresolved non-runtime resume-gate schema mismatch instead of narrowing the comparison to common keys, preserving the existing exact-match gate for full payload diffs.
prevention: Resume compatibility checks must fail closed on non-runtime schema drift between recorded and current payloads.
Never reduce a fingerprint comparison to shared keys unless a narrowly defined compatibility rule reconstructs the missing field first.
Keep regression coverage for both successful same-stage resume and denied resume when a recorded semantic field disappears from the recomputed payload.
notes: This pass also hardened EM and production sentinel-ordering regressions without changing the already-fixed sentinel implementation.
